note: Canonical Crave Score color tiers for the 0-10 display scale. Ten deciles: tier i covers rating [i, i+1). `default` is the V3 ramp DARKENED 5%: the V3 ramp is a perceptually OKLab-spaced gradient anchored to three colors the owner sampled from macOS system surfaces with Digital Color Meter in DISPLAY-NATIVE (Display-P3) mode (red, gold, Apple system green #35C759), converted P3->sRGB; at the owner's request every tier is then scaled to 95% HSV value (brightness down 5%, hue + saturation held EXACTLY constant -- deeper, not muted), so each tier is a slightly darker version of the originally-picked color (e.g. the green anchor #35C759 -> #32BD55). The vivid-looking hex values are sRGB encodings; on a wide-gamut (P3) screen they render softer. To re-darken/re-lighten, re-scale the ORIGINAL V3 ramp's HSV value, not these already-darkened values. `colorblind` is a viridis ramp for the future accessibility toggle. quality-color.ts and the scripts/generate-*-sprites.js generators BOTH read this file so pin sprites, dots, and rank pills stay in lockstep. Do not hand-edit colors anywhere else.
